heading: Wind energy research and development
label: 16237 Wind energy research and development
notes: CodificationSection was enacted as part of the Energy Act of 2020, and not as part of the Energy Policy Act of 2005 which comprises this chapter.

Application, , , provided that:Pub. L. 116–260, div. Z, title IX, § 9006(b)134 Stat. 2600Dec. 27, 2020“The provisions of section 602 of the Public Works and Economic Development Act of 1965 () shall apply with respect to construction, alteration, or repair work of demonstration projects funded by grants or contracts authorized under sections 3001 [enacting part C of subchapter V of chapter 152 of this title], 3003 [enacting this section], 3004 [enacting ], 5001 [enacting ], and 8007 [enacting ] and the amendments made by such sections.”42 U.S.C. 3212section 16238 of this titlesection 16298d of this titlesection 17389 of this title

children.heading: Critical material
children.label: 1 Critical material
children.text: The term “critical material” has the meaning given the term in .section 1606 of title 30

children.heading: Economically distressed area
children.label: 2 Economically distressed area
children.text: The term “economically distressed area” means an area described in .section 3161(a) of this title

children.label: M
children.text: a consortium of 2 or more entities described in subparagraphs (A) through (L).

children.heading: Indian Tribe
children.label: 4 Indian Tribe
children.text: The term “Indian Tribe” has the meaning given the term in .section 5304 of title 25

children.label: A

children.heading: Minority serving institution
children.label: 6 Minority serving institution
children.text: The term “minority-serving institution” has the meaning given the term “eligible institution” in .section 1067q(a) of title 20

children.heading: National Laboratory
children.label: 7 National Laboratory
children.text: The term “National Laboratory” has the meaning given such term in .section 15801(3) of this title

children.heading: Native Hawaiian community-based organization
children.label: 8 Native Hawaiian community-based organization
children.text: The term “Native Hawaiian community-based organization” has the meaning given the term in .section 7517 of title 20

children.heading: Program
children.label: 9 Program
children.text: The term “program” means the program established under subsection (b)(1).

children.heading: Secretary
children.label: 10 Secretary
children.text: The term “Secretary” means the Secretary of Energy.

children.heading: Territory or freely associated state
children.label: 11 Territory or freely associated state
children.text: The term “territory or freely associated state” has the meaning given the term “insular area” in .section 3103 of title 7

children.heading: Tribal energy development organization
children.label: 12 Tribal energy development organization
children.text: The term “Tribal energy development organization” has the meaning given the term “tribal energy development organization” in .section 3501 of title 25

children.heading: Tribal organization
children.label: 13 Tribal organization
children.text: The term “Tribal organization” has the meaning given the term in .section 5304 of title 25

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary shall establish a program to conduct research, development, demonstration, and commercialization of wind energy technologies in accordance with this subsection.

children.label: i
children.text: To improve the energy efficiency, cost effectiveness, reliability, resilience, security, siting, integration, manufacturability, installation, decommissioning, and recyclability of wind energy technologies.

children.label: ii
children.text: To optimize the performance and operation of wind energy components, turbines, and systems, including through the development of new materials, hardware, and software.

children.label: I
children.text: at varying hub heights; and

children.label: II
children.text: through the use of computer modeling.

children.label: iv
children.text: To support the integration of wind energy technologies with the electric grid and other energy technologies and systems.

children.label: I
children.text: manufacturing, siting, permitting, installation, operations, maintenance, decommissioning, and recycling; and

children.label: II
children.text: through the development of solutions to transportation barriers to wind components.

children.label: vi
children.text: To reduce and mitigate potential negative impacts of wind energy technologies on human communities, the environment, or commerce.

children.label: vii
children.text: To address barriers to the commercialization and export of wind energy technologies.

children.label: viii
children.text: To support the domestic wind industry, workforce, and supply chain.

children.heading: Targets
children.label: C Targets
children.text: Not later than 180 days after , the Secretary shall establish targets for the program relating to near-term (up to 2 years), mid-term (up to 7 years), and long-term (up to 15 years) challenges to the advancement of wind energy technologies, including onshore, offshore, distributed, and off-grid technologies.December 27, 2020

children.label: i
children.text: awarding grants and awards, on a competitive, merit-reviewed basis;

children.label: ii
children.text: performing precompetitive research and development;

children.label: iii
children.text: establishing or maintaining demonstration facilities and projects, including through stewardship of existing facilities such as the National Wind Test Center;

children.label: iv
children.text: providing technical assistance;

children.label: v
children.text: entering into contracts and cooperative agreements;

children.label: vi
children.text: providing small business vouchers;

children.label: vii
children.text: establishing prize competitions;

children.label: viii
children.text: conducting education and outreach activities;

children.label: ix
children.text: conducting professional development activities; and

children.label: x
children.text: conducting analyses, studies, and reports.

children.label: i
children.text: Wind power plant siting, performance, operations, and security.

children.label: ii
children.text: New materials and designs relating to all hardware, software, and components of wind energy technologies, including technologies and strategies that reduce the use of energy, water, critical materials, and other commodities that are determined to be vulnerable to disruption.

children.label: iii
children.text: Advanced wind energy manufacturing and installation technologies and practices, including materials, processes, such as onsite or near site manufacturing, and design.

children.label: I
children.text: fixed and floating substructure systems, materials, and components;

children.label: aa
children.text: an offshore research facility to conduct research for oceanic, biological, geological, and atmospheric resource characterization relevant to offshore wind energy development in coordination with the ocean and atmospheric science communities; and

children.label: bb
children.text: an offshore support structure testing facility to conduct development, demonstration, and commercialization of large-scale and full-scale offshore wind energy support structure components and systems;

children.label: III
children.text: the monitoring and analysis of site and environmental considerations unique to offshore sites, including freshwater environments.

children.label: I
children.text: the electric grid, including transmission, distribution, microgrids, and distributed energy systems; and

children.label: aa
children.text: other generation sources;

children.label: bb
children.text: demand response technologies; and

children.label: cc
children.text: energy storage technologies.

children.label: vi
children.text: Methods to improve the lifetime, maintenance, decommissioning, recycling, reuse, and sustainability of wind energy components and systems, including technologies and strategies to reduce the use of energy, water, critical materials, and other valuable or harmful inputs.

children.label: vii
children.text: Wind power forecasting and atmospheric measurement systems, including for turbines and plant systems of varying height.

children.label: I
children.text: generation sources;

children.label: II
children.text: loads; and

children.label: III
children.text: storage technologies.

children.label: I
children.text: distributed wind technologies, including the development of best practices, models, and voluntary streamlined processes for local siting and permitting of distributed wind energy systems to reduce costs;

children.label: II
children.text: airspace;

children.label: III
children.text: military operations;

children.label: IV
children.text: radar;

children.label: V
children.text: local communities, with special consideration given to economically distressed areas, previously disturbed lands such as landfills and former mines, and other areas disproportionately impacted by environmental pollution;

children.label: VI
children.text: wildlife and wildlife habitats; and

children.label: VII
children.text: any other appropriate matter, as determined by the Secretary.

children.label: x
children.text: Technologies or strategies to avoid, minimize, and offset the potential impacts of wind energy facilities on bird species, bat species, marine wildlife, and other sensitive species and habitats.

children.label: xi
children.text: Advanced physics-based and data analysis computational tools, in coordination with the high-performance computing programs of the Department, to more efficiently design, site, permit, manufacture, install, operate, decommission, and recycle wind energy systems.

children.label: xii
children.text: Technologies for distributed wind, including micro, small, and medium turbines and the components of those turbines and their microgrid applications.

children.label: xiii
children.text: Transformational technologies for harnessing wind energy.

children.label: xiv
children.text: Other research areas that advance the purposes of the program, as determined by the Secretary.

children.label: I
children.text: are located in a geographically diverse range of eligible entities;

children.label: aa
children.text: in economically distressed areas and areas disproportionately impacted by pollution; and

children.label: bb
children.text: that provide the greatest potential to reduce energy costs, as well as promote accessibility and community implementation of demonstrated technologies;

children.label: III
children.text: can be replicated in a variety of regions and climates;

children.label: aa
children.text: domestic manufacturing and production of wind energy technologies; or

children.label: bb
children.text: exports of wind energy technologies; and

children.label: V
children.text: are carried out in collaboration with Tribal energy development organizations, Indian Tribes, Tribal organizations, Native Hawaiian community-based organizations, minority-serving institutions, or territories or freely associated States; and

children.label: ii
children.text: with regards to professional development, activities that expand the number of individuals from underrepresented groups pursuing and attaining skills relevant to wind energy.

children.heading: Coordination
children.label: D Coordination
children.text: To the maximum extent practicable, the Secretary shall coordinate activities under the program with other relevant programs and capabilities of the Department and other Federal research programs.

children.heading: Use of funds
children.label: E Use of funds
children.text: To the extent that funding is not otherwise available through other Federal programs or power purchase agreements, funding awarded for demonstration projects may be used for additional nontechnology costs, as determined to be appropriate by the Secretary, such as engineering or feasibility studies.

children.heading: Solicitation
children.label: F Solicitation
children.text: Not less than once every two years, the Secretary shall conduct a national solicitation for applications for demonstration projects under this section.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Not later than 180 days after , the Secretary shall submit to the Committee on Science, Space, and Technology of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Energy and Natural Resources of the Senate a report on the potential for, and technical viability of, airborne wind energy systems to provide a significant source of energy in the United States.December 27, 2020

children.heading: Contents
children.label: ii Contents
children.text: The report under paragraph (1) shall include a summary of research, development, demonstration, and commercialization needs, including an estimate of Federal funding requirements, to further examine and validate the technical and economic viability of airborne wind energy concepts over the 10-year period beginning on .December 27, 2020

children.heading: Wind technician training grant program
children.label: 3 Wind technician training grant program
children.text: The Secretary may award grants, on a competitive basis, to eligible entities to purchase large pieces of wind component equipment, such as nacelles, towers, and blades, for use in training wind technician students in onshore or offshore wind applications.

children.heading: Wind energy technology recycling research, development, and demonstration program
children.label: 4 Wind energy technology recycling research, development, and demonstration program
i
children.text: by increasing the efficiency and cost effectiveness of the recovery of raw materials from wind energy technology components and systems, including enabling technologies such as inverters;

children.label: ii
children.text: by minimizing potential environmental impacts from the recovery and disposal processes;

children.label: iii
children.text: by advancing technologies and processes for the disassembly and recycling of wind energy devices;

children.label: iv
children.text: by developing alternative materials, designs, manufacturing processes, and other aspects of wind energy technologies and the disassembly and resource recovery process that enable efficient, cost effective, and environmentally responsible disassembly of, and resource recovery from, wind energy technologies; and

children.label: v
children.text: strategies to increase consumer acceptance of, and participation in, the recycling of wind energy technologies.

children.label: i
children.text: development of best practices or training materials for use in the wind energy technology manufacturing, design, installation, decommissioning, or recycling industries;

children.label: ii
children.text: dissemination at industry conferences;

children.label: iii
children.text: coordination with information dissemination programs relating to recycling of electronic devices in general;

children.label: iv
children.text: demonstration projects; and

children.label: v
children.text: educational materials.

children.heading: Priority
children.label: C Priority
children.text: In carrying out the activities authorized under this subsection, the Secretary shall give special consideration to projects that recover critical materials.

children.heading: Sensitive information
children.label: D Sensitive information
children.text: In carrying out the activities authorized under this subsection, the Secretary shall ensure proper security controls are in place to protect proprietary or sensitive information, as appropriate.

children.heading: Wind energy technology materials physical property database
children.label: 5 Wind energy technology materials physical property database

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Not later than , the Secretary shall establish a comprehensive physical property database of materials for use in wind energy technologies, which shall identify the type, quantity, country of origin, source, significant uses, projected availability, and physical properties of materials used in wind energy technologies.September 1, 2022

children.label: i
children.text: other Department activities, including those carried out by the Office of Science;

children.label: ii
children.text: the Director of the National Institute of Standards and Technology;

children.label: iii
children.text: the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency;

children.label: iv
children.text: the Secretary of the Interior; and

children.label: v
children.text: relevant industry stakeholders, as determined by the Secretary.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Not later than , and every 6 years thereafter, the Secretary shall submit to Congress a report on the strategic vision, progress, goals, and targets of the program, including assessments of wind energy markets and manufacturing.September 1, 2022

children.label: i
children.text: existing peer review processes;

children.label: ii
children.text: studies conducted by the National Laboratories; and

children.label: iii
children.text: the multiyear program planning required under .section 16358 of this title

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: 7 Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There is authorized to be appropriated to the Secretary to carry out the program $125,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2021 through 2025.
